plan_adherence_notes: Plan steps 1 and 2 were executed in backend/server.py and tests/test_bayes_market_api.py: the branch adds normalized status/sort/q parsing, status=all aliasing, trimmed case-insensitive title search, descending sort modes, expanded meta.filters, and backend coverage for aliases, duplicates, search, combined filters, and sort order. Plan steps 3 and 4 were not executed: frontend/src/lib/api/client.ts still only sends status, frontend/src/lib/query/hooks.ts still keys/fetches only by status, and frontend/src/routes/MarketList.tsx still exposes only the existing status dropdown with no sort or search controls. Step 5 was only partial: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_bayes_market_api.py' passed (213 tests), but frontend verification could not run in this checkout because frontend/node_modules is missing and pnpm test failed with 'vitest: Permission denied'.
missed_questions: If frontend delivery is in scope, what exact MarketList UI contract should ship for sort and q, including whether the current empty-string 'All statuses' option should become an explicit status=all request?
What frontend acceptance tests are required to prove the client, hooks, and MarketList route actually send the new status/sort/q params end to end?
reason: The research and plan are coherent, and the backend implementation itself looks correct and is covered by passing backend tests. The branch still falls short of the approved plan because the frontend client/hooks/MarketList path was not updated, so users cannot exercise the new sort and search parameters from the UI and the end-to-end scope described by the plan is incomplete.
